basic_details.job_title: NIACL Apprentices Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 550 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The New India Assurance Company Limited (NIACL), a government-owned general insurance giant under the Ministry of Finance, has officially launched its Apprentice Engagement Drive for 2026–27. This recruitment is conducted under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) and the Apprentices Act, 1961 - making it a legitimate, government-recognized training opportunity for fresh graduates across India.

application_fee.details: All fees must be paid online. No offline payment mode is accepted. GST at 18% is charged over the base fee. | Once paid, the fee is non-refundable under all circumstances, including application rejection or withdrawal.

age_limit.details: Category Minimum Age Maximum Age General / EWS 21 years 30 years OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 21 years 33 years SC / ST 21 years 35 years PwBD 21 years 40 years Widows / Divorced Women (General/EWS) - 35 years Widows / Divorced Women (OBC) - 38 years Widows / Divorced Women (SC/ST) - 40 years | Date of birth range for General/EWS candidates: Not earlier than June 2, 1996, and not later than June 1, 2005.

qualification.details: Understanding the eligibility criteria is the first and most critical step before applying. Here is a detailed breakdown of all conditions you must satisfy. | All applicants must be citizens of India. | Candidates must hold a graduate degree in any discipline from a recognized university, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. | Crucially, the final year mark sheet date must fall between January 1, 2022 and June 1, 2026 (both dates inclusive). Graduates who passed before January 1, 2022, or after June 1, 2026, are not eligible. | Candidates must not have previously undergone apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act 1961 - either with NIACL or any other organization. Candidates currently pursuing an apprenticeship anywhere are also ineligible. Candidates with one or more years of job experience after graduation are not eligible. | Date of birth range for General/EWS candidates: Not earlier than June 2, 1996, and not later than June 1, 2005.

salary_details.details: This section covers what apprentices receive during training and the rules governing their engagement. | Monthly stipend: ₹12,300 Payment is made by the 10th day of the following month No additional benefits, allowances, or statutory contributions (PF, Mediclaim, etc.) are provided Pro-rata deduction applies for unauthorized absences | 1 day of leave per month, credited at the beginning of the next month Leave can be carried forward but not more than 2 days at a time All accumulated leave lapses at the end of the contract Apprentices are entitled to all company-observed public holidays | Training period: 12 months On completion, apprentices undergo an assessment (theory by BFSI SSC + practical by NIACL) Successful candidates receive an Apprenticeship Certificate from BFSI SSC | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now | It is important to note clearly: this is not a job offer. NIACL is under no obligation to offer employment after training, and apprentices have no right to claim a job. This is a training engagement, not a contract or regular employment.

using your own camera-enabled device - desktop
laptop

or smartphone.
Duration: 60 minutes Tie-breaking rule: If two candidates score equally
the older candidate is ranked higher (descending age order).
Candidates who clear the online written test are shortlisted in 3× the number of available seats per state per category for this stage.
Candidates who studied the regional language of their chosen state in Class 10 or 12 are exempt from the language test. All others must demonstrate proficiency (reading
writing

and understanding) in the specified local language of the state. Candidates failing the language test cannot be engaged as apprentices.
Carry originals plus self-attested photocopies of:

Photo ID proof (Aadhaar / PAN / Voter ID / Driving License / Passport) Class 10 certificate (as proof of date of birth) Class 12 marksheets and graduation marksheets (semester/year-wise) with degree CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate (if applicable) SC / ST / OBC / EWS / PwBD certificates with required declarations
how_to_apply: Here is a clear walkthrough of exactly what you need to do to successfully submit your application.
Gather these before starting the registration to avoid interruptions:
Valid personal email ID (for account activation and login)
Qualifying degree or provisional certificate (PDF format, under 1 MB)

1. Register on NATS Portal Visit https://nats.education.gov.in, create your account, and ensure your profile is 100% complete. Then apply specifically for The New India Assurance Company Limited's apprenticeship listing.
2. Note Your NATS Enrolment Number After applying on NATS, you will receive a unique NATS enrolment number. This is mandatory for the next step.

3. Apply on BFSI SSC Portal Go to https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_login. Fill in personal details, upload documents, enter your NATS enrolment number, choose your preferred state, and pay the examination fee.
4. Select Only One State Candidates can apply for only one state. Your training, if selected, will be at a Regional Office within that state based on the company's requirements.
5. Confirm Payment & Save Application After successful payment, take a printout of your completed online application form. No changes are permitted after final submission.
important_instructions: Certain candidate groups have specific rules they must be aware of before applying.

Only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates are entitled to OBC reservation benefits. Creamy layer OBC candidates must apply under the General (Unreserved) category and meet General category age criteria. An undertaking regarding non-creamy layer status must be submitted at the time of fee payment.

Candidates must produce an Income and Asset Certificate for financial year 2025–26, issued by a competent authority after March 31, 2026. Gross annual family income must be below ₹8 lakh, and the family must not own 5+ acres of agricultural land, 1000+ sq. ft. residential flat, or qualifying residential plots.

Minimum disability of 40% is required for reservation benefits. Eligible disability types: Visual Impairment (VI), Hearing Impairment (HI), Locomotor/Orthopedic (OH), Intellectual/Autism (ID), Multiple Disabilities (MD). PwBD candidates using a scribe get 20 minutes of compensatory time per hour. The scribe must not be a co-applicant for the same exam and cannot serve as scribe for more than one candidate.

rows.information: BFSI Sector Skill Council of India

content_sections.heading: NIACL Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: The official NIACL Apprentice Recruitment 2026 notification PDF contains all details including category-wise vacancies, state-wise seat distribution, certificate formats for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD candidates, and guidelines for the selection process. Download the Official NIACL Apprentice 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Important Dates - NIACL Apprentice 2026 Timeline
content_sections.text: Never miss a deadline. Mark these dates on your calendar.

content_sections.heading: Who Can Apply? - NIACL Apprentice 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Understanding the eligibility criteria is the first and most critical step before applying. Here is a detailed breakdown of all conditions you must satisfy. All applicants must be citizens of India . Candidates must hold a graduate degree in any discipline from a recognized university, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. Crucially, the final year mark sheet date must fall between January 1, 2022 and June 1, 2026 (both dates inclusive). Graduates who passed before January 1, 2022, or after June 1, 2026, are not eligible . Candidates must not have previously undergone apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act 1961 - either with NIACL or any other organization. Candidates currently pursuing an apprenticeship anywhere are also ineligible. Candidates with one or more years of job experience after graduation are not eligible. Date of birth range for General/EWS candidates: Not earlier than June 2, 1996, and not later than June 1, 2005.

content_sections.heading: NIACL Apprentice 2026 - State-wise & Category-wise Vacancy Breakdown
content_sections.text: The 550 training seats are distributed across states and Union Territories, with reservations for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, General, and PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) candidates. Here are the major states by seat count. Seats in smaller states and UTs (such as Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Arunachal Pradesh, Goa, Himachal Pradesh, etc.) have 1 seat each in the General category. Seat numbers are tentative and may change based on actual company requirements. PwBD reservations are allocated horizontally across categories. A total of 43 PwBD seats are available nationally, covering: VI (Visual Impairment): 36 seats HI (Hearing Impairment): 5 seats OH (Orthopedic/Locomotor): 1 seat ID/MD (Intellectual/Multiple Disabilities): 1 seat

content_sections.heading: NIACL Apprentices Recruitment 2026 Apply Online Link - Activated
content_sections.text: Online Applications Are Now Open (June 23 – July 6, 2026) The application process is a two-step procedure. Both steps must be completed for your application to be valid. Step 1 - Register on the NATS Portal: Apply via the official NATS (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme) portal run by the Government of India: https://nats.education.gov.in (Complete 100% of your profile and click "APPLY" against The New India Assurance Company Limited.) Step 2 - Complete Application & Pay Fee on BFSI Portal: After NATS registration, proceed to: https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_login (Update your personal information, NATS enrolment number, documents, state preference, and pay the examination fee.) Important: Your application is only considered complete after the examination fee is successfully paid online. Incomplete applications will be automatically rejected.

content_sections.heading: Application & Examination Fee Structure
content_sections.text: All fees must be paid online. No offline payment mode is accepted. GST at 18% is charged over the base fee. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable under all circumstances, including application rejection or withdrawal.

content_sections.heading: Step-by-Step Application Process for NIACL Apprentice 2026
content_sections.text: Here is a clear walkthrough of exactly what you need to do to successfully submit your application. Gather these before starting the registration to avoid interruptions: Aadhaar Card Valid personal email ID (for account activation and login) Active mobile number Passport-size photograph (JPEG format, under 1 MB) Qualifying degree or provisional certificate (PDF format, under 1 MB) 1. Register on NATS Portal Visit https://nats.education.gov.in , create your account, and ensure your profile is 100% complete . Then apply specifically for The New India Assurance Company Limited's apprenticeship listing. 2. Note Your NATS Enrolment Number After applying on NATS, you will receive a unique NATS enrolment number. This is mandatory for the next step. 3. Apply on BFSI SSC Portal Go to https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_login . Fill in personal details, upload documents, enter your NATS enrolment number, choose your preferred state, and pay the examination fee. 4. Select Only One State Candidates can apply for only one state . Your training, if selected, will be at a Regional Office within that state based on the company's requirements. 5. Confirm Payment & Save Application After successful payment, take a printout of your completed online application form. No changes are permitted after final submission.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process - How NIACL Apprentices Are Chosen
content_sections.text: Selection happens in two main stages after fee payment. Here is what to expect at each stage. The online exam is held from home (proctored), using your own camera-enabled device - desktop, laptop, tablet, or smartphone. Duration: 60 minutes Tie-breaking rule: If two candidates score equally, the older candidate is ranked higher (descending age order). Candidates who clear the online written test are shortlisted in 3× the number of available seats per state per category for this stage. Candidates who studied the regional language of their chosen state in Class 10 or 12 are exempt from the language test. All others must demonstrate proficiency (reading, writing, speaking, and understanding) in the specified local language of the state. Candidates failing the language test cannot be engaged as apprentices . Carry originals plus self-attested photocopies of: Photo ID proof (Aadhaar / PAN / Voter ID / Driving License / Passport) Class 10 certificate (as proof of date of birth) Class 12 marksheets and graduation marksheets (semester/year-wise) with degree CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate (if applicable) SC / ST / OBC / EWS / PwBD certificates with required declarations

content_sections.heading: Stipend, Training Conditions & What to Expect
content_sections.text: This section covers what apprentices receive during training and the rules governing their engagement. Monthly stipend: ₹12,300 Payment is made by the 10th day of the following month No additional benefits, allowances, or statutory contributions (PF, Mediclaim, etc.) are provided Pro-rata deduction applies for unauthorized absences 1 day of leave per month , credited at the beginning of the next month Leave can be carried forward but not more than 2 days at a time All accumulated leave lapses at the end of the contract Apprentices are entitled to all company-observed public holidays Training period: 12 months On completion, apprentices undergo an assessment (theory by BFSI SSC + practical by NIACL) Successful candidates receive an Apprenticeship Certificate from BFSI SSC Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now It is important to note clearly: this is not a job offer . NIACL is under no obligation to offer employment after training, and apprentices have no right to claim a job. This is a training engagement, not a contract or regular employment.

content_sections.heading: Regional Offices & State Mapping - Where Will You Be Trained?
content_sections.text: NIACL has 28 Regional Offices across India. Your training location depends on the state you select during application and the company's seat availability. Here are some key state-to-regional-office mappings:

rows.states_covered: Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Andaman & Nicobar Islands
Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Manipur, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura
Chandigarh UT, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, Leh

content_sections.heading: Special Guidelines for Reserved Category Candidates
content_sections.text: Certain candidate groups have specific rules they must be aware of before applying. Only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates are entitled to OBC reservation benefits. Creamy layer OBC candidates must apply under the General (Unreserved) category and meet General category age criteria. An undertaking regarding non-creamy layer status must be submitted at the time of fee payment. Candidates must produce an Income and Asset Certificate for financial year 2025–26, issued by a competent authority after March 31, 2026. Gross annual family income must be below ₹8 lakh , and the family must not own 5+ acres of agricultural land, 1000+ sq. ft. residential flat, or qualifying residential plots. Minimum disability of 40% is required for reservation benefits. Eligible disability types: Visual Impairment (VI), Hearing Impairment (HI), Locomotor/Orthopedic (OH), Intellectual/Autism (ID), Multiple Disabilities (MD). PwBD candidates using a scribe get 20 minutes of compensatory time per hour . The scribe must not be a co-applicant for the same exam and cannot serve as scribe for more than one candidate.

faq.question: Q1. What is NIACL Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: NIACL Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a 12-month apprenticeship training program offered by The New India Assurance Company Limited under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) and the Apprentices Act, 1961. It offers 550 training seats across India with a monthly stipend of ₹12,300.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The closing date for online registration and fee payment is July 6, 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be Indian citizens, graduates (any discipline) with final year results dated between January 1, 2022 and June 1, 2026, aged between 21 and 30 years (General/EWS) as of June 1, 2026, with no prior apprenticeship or job experience exceeding one year.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend for NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Selected apprentices receive ₹12,300 per month, paid by the 10th of the following month. No other benefits or allowances are provided.

faq.question: Q5. Is NIACL Apprentice 2026 a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. This is strictly an apprenticeship training program. NIACL is not obligated to offer employment after training, and apprentices cannot claim any right to employment.

faq.question: Q6. How many vacancies are there in NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: There are 550 training seats distributed across all states and union territories, with reservations for SC (64), ST (32), OBC (114), EWS (43), and General (297) categories.

faq.question: Q7. What is the exam pattern for NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The online test has 100 questions across 4 sections — General/Financial Awareness, General English, Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude, and Computer Knowledge — all for 100 marks, completed in 60 minutes.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for NIACL Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: General/EWS/OBC male candidates pay ₹944 (₹800 + 18% GST). Female candidates in these categories pay ₹708. SC/ST/PwBD candidates pay ₹236 regardless of gender.
